name: Baked Sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
May be frozen. Defrost in the fridge, on the way to a picnic, or in the microwave if you're short on time.
1. In a mixer bowl, combine water and yeast. Let stand for 5 minutes.
2. Add flour and mix at medium speed for another 4 minutes.
3. Turn dough onto a lightly floured board and knead briefly. Form into a ball and place in a well-greased mixing bowl. Grease the top, cover with a cloth, and let rise until doubled in volume.
4. In a large skillet or pot, cook ground beef and onions over medium heat, stirring frequently, until onions are soft and meat is browned. Drain excess fat and juice, then return meat and onions to the pan. Add tomato sauce, catsup, garlic salt, oregano, and pepper. Cook until meat is dry with no visible liquid. Set aside to cool.
5. After dough has risen, transfer to a lightly floured surface. Knead lightly. Divide into 12 equal portions. Form each portion into a small ball, cover with a cloth, and let rest for 10 minutes.
6. Roll each ball into a circle about 5-6 inches across. Place about 1/4 cup meat mixture in the center of the circle. Fold dough up around the filling and press together at the top. Place on a well-greased cookie sheet, cover with a cloth, and let rise until doubled in volume (about 30-40 minutes).
7. Bake at 350°F for 40-45 minutes or until lightly browned and firm.
Serving: Serve hot, using one sandwich per serving, or refrigerate or freeze for later use. Each sandwich contains approximately 293 calories, 27 grams carbohydrates, 21 grams protein, 9 grams fat, and 574 milligrams sodium. For low-sodium diets, omit salt and garlic salt, use low-sodium tomato sauce and catsup, and add 1/4 teaspoon powdered garlic. For low-cholesterol diets, omit the egg and substitute with 2 egg whites or 1/4 cup liquid egg substitute.
